title: South Korea to Command RIMPAC Naval Forces for First Time
summary: Republic of Korea Navy will lead Combined Naval Forces at RIMPAC for the first time in the exercise's 55-year history.
contents:
# South Korea to Command RIMPAC Naval Forces for First Time

## Overview
Republic of Korea Navy will lead Combined Naval Forces at RIMPAC for the first time in the exercise's 55-year history.

## Details
The ROK Navy brigadier general admiral will assume the role of Combined Task Force commander, overseeing operations from about 30 countries with around 40 surface ships and submarines. US Navy 3rd Fleet announced the multinational exercise will run from late June to July in Hawaii. This marks a historic shift as South Korea takes operational control of the world's largest multinational maritime drill.[2]

## Location
Hawaii, United States

## Source Context
Reporting referenced from coverage tracked by the @KC_NWT OSINT beat. The cited outlet provides additional context, attribution, and verification of the claims summarized above.

## Tags
RIMPAC, South Korea Navy, multinational exercise, US allies, Hawaii drill, naval command
